title: Houses for Rent in Buckeye, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Buckeye, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Houses for rent in Buckeye, Cleveland — a Section 8-friendly east-side neighborhood near Woodland Hills Park and Shaker Square. Book a free showing today.
answerBox: Buckeye — formally the Buckeye-Woodhill area — sits on Cleveland's east side around Woodhill Road and Woodland Hills Park, just north of Shaker Square. Two- and three-bedroom rental homes here commonly run from about $750 to $1,300 a month, and Rent Finder Cleveland manages voucher-friendly homes throughout the neighborhood.

body: Cleveland's Buckeye neighborhood — officially the **Buckeye-Woodhill Statistical Planning Area** in the city's own planning framework — sits on the east side centered around Woodhill Road and Woodland Hills Park, directly north of Shaker Square. The area's housing stock is a mix of early-to-mid-20th-century single-family homes and duplexes, many with front porches and small side yards, on tree-lined residential streets running off Woodhill Road and Woodland Avenue.

Rent Finder Cleveland manages voucher-friendly rental homes throughout this part of Cleveland's east side. Every home we manage accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see what's currently available, or tell our team what you're looking for.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent in Buckeye?
body: Rents in the Buckeye area generally track Cleveland's more affordable east-side neighborhoods rather than the higher-priced professionally-managed buildings closer to University Circle. Two-bedroom homes typically run from the mid-$700s to around $1,000 a month, and three-bedroom homes from about $1,000 to $1,300. For context, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report put the Cleveland-wide 2-bedroom median at $1,100/month and the 3-bedroom median at $1,350/month — Buckeye rentals generally sit at or below those citywide figures, reflecting the neighborhood's older, smaller-scale housing stock.

Buckeye is bordered by Shaker Square just to the south, where GCRTA's Blue and Green light-rail lines run along Shaker Boulevard toward Tower City downtown. Woodland Avenue and Woodhill Road carry regular GCRTA bus routes, and I-77 is a short drive away for trips downtown or toward the southern suburbs. Woodland Hills Park gives the neighborhood direct access to green space and walking paths.

items: **Location:** East side, centered on Woodhill Road, just north of Shaker Square
**Transit:** GCRTA bus routes on Woodland Avenue/Woodhill Road; Blue and Green light rail nearby at Shaker Square
**Driving:** Close to I-77 for downtown and southern-suburb commutes

body: Buckeye is one of the east-side Cleveland neighborhoods where Rent Finder Cleveland concentrates its voucher-friendly rental homes. Every property we manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. A voucher itself comes from the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA), which accepts preliminary applications year-round through its online portal; selection then runs through periodic lottery draws rather than strict first-come, first-served order. Because Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and Cleveland does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance, it's still worth confirming acceptance directly with any owner outside our managed portfolio. Read our full [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the details on applying and payment standards.

Buckeye's housing stock and price range are similar to nearby east-side neighborhoods like [Slavic Village](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-slavic-village-cleveland/) and [Hough](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/), both also part of our managed footprint. All three neighborhoods share a similar early-to-mid-20th-century housing stock of single-family homes and duplexes, so renters weighing Buckeye against a neighboring area are usually comparing commute distance and specific street condition rather than a large difference in home type or price. If you'd like to compare options across the whole city rather than one neighborhood at a time, our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) hub lets you browse by budget, bedroom count, and area, and [applying online](/apply) takes just a few minutes once you've found a home you like.

faq.q: Is Buckeye the same as Buckeye-Shaker Square?
faq.a: They're related but distinct. "Buckeye" generally refers to the Buckeye-Woodhill area centered on Woodhill Road and Woodland Hills Park, while "Buckeye-Shaker Square" refers to the district directly around the Shaker Square light-rail stop just to the south. Both sit on Cleveland's east side.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Buckeye?
faq.a: Yes. Rent Finder Cleveland manages voucher-friendly rental homes throughout the Buckeye area. Every home we manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Book a showing to see current availability.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Buckeye?
faq.a: Two-bedroom homes typically rent from the mid-$700s to about $1,000 a month, and three-bedroom homes from roughly $1,000 to $1,300 — generally at or below Cleveland's citywide medians reported by Zumper for July 2026.

faq.q: What transit serves the Buckeye neighborhood?
faq.a: GCRTA bus routes run along Woodland Avenue and Woodhill Road, and the Blue and Green light-rail lines are accessible just south at Shaker Square, connecting to Tower City and downtown Cleveland.

faq.q: What kind of houses are available in Buckeye?
faq.a: Most homes are early-to-mid-20th-century single-family houses and duplexes on residential side streets off Woodhill Road, typically two or three bedrooms with a basement and a small yard.
